stories.headline: US could 'finish the job' in Iran in two or three weeks, Trump says
stories.summary: Donald Trump claims the United States could complete military operations against Iran within two to three weeks, suggesting an accelerated timeline for potential conflict in the region. The statement comes amid ongoing tensions involving Iranian-aligned groups, including reported joint attacks by Houthis alongside Iranian and Hezbollah forces against Israel.

stories.headline: US journalist Shelly Kittleson kidnapped in Baghdad
stories.summary: Shelly Kittleson, a U.S. journalist working in Baghdad, has been kidnapped, with Iraqi security forces launching efforts to locate her abductors. The incident marks a rare abduction of a Western journalist in Iraq's capital in recent years, raising concerns about security conditions for foreign press operating in the country.

stories.headline: Trump expected to attend supreme court arguments on landmark birthright citizenship case - US politics live
stories.summary: The Supreme Court hears arguments on whether the government can restrict birthright citizenship—a constitutional right that automatically grants citizenship to those born on U.S. soil—in a case that could affect millions of people and reshape immigration policy. Trump plans to attend the proceedings, signaling the high political stakes of a ruling that would require overturning more than a century of legal precedent.

stories.headline: Judge temporarily halts construction of Trump's White House ballroom
stories.summary: A federal judge has issued a temporary halt to construction of a new ballroom at the White House, citing unresolved legal questions about the project's authority and scope. Trump has indicated he intends to continue the work despite the court order, setting up a potential clash over judicial authority and executive prerogative.

stories.headline: What is NASA’s Artemis II moon mission, and when will it launch?
stories.summary: Artemis II will carry four astronauts—three American and one Canadian—on a crewed lunar flyby mission that orbits the moon without landing, serving as a test flight for the larger Artemis III landing mission planned for later this decade. The launch represents a major milestone in NASA's effort to return humans to the moon and establish a sustained presence there, with significant implications for U.S. leadership in space exploration.

stories.headline: Israel intensifies Lebanon attacks and hits areas not in Hezbollah's control
stories.summary: Israeli airstrikes in the Beirut area have killed at least seven people, including a Hezbollah commander responsible for Iraq operations, in attacks that extend beyond areas traditionally controlled by the militant group. Ground fighting continues in southern Lebanon as Israeli forces advance and Hezbollah resists the incursion.

stories.headline: Iran attacks cause fire in Kuwait, Bahrain; kill man in UAE
stories.summary: Iran's attacks across the Persian Gulf region have struck infrastructure and vessels, including fuel tanks at Kuwait's airport and an oil tanker near Dubai, with one death reported in the UAE. The escalating exchanges involve U.S. strikes on Isfahan and damage visible across both Iranian and targeted facilities in the region.

stories.headline: Tiger Woods to 'step away and seek treatment' after crash
stories.summary: Tiger Woods announces he will step away from professional golf to seek treatment following his arrest on driving under the influence charges related to a recent car crash. He has pleaded not guilty to the charge and will miss upcoming tournaments, including the Masters.

stories.headline: The World Cup line-up is complete - here's what you need to know
stories.summary: The 2026 FIFA World Cup will feature 48 teams instead of the traditional 32, with qualification now complete across all regions. Africa qualifies a record 10 nations for the tournament, reflecting the expanded format's broader geographic reach.

stories.headline: Russian military plane crash in Crimea kills at least 29
stories.summary: A Russian military transport plane crashed in Crimea, killing at least 29 people on board. The incident occurred as Russia faces ongoing logistical pressures in the region amid its military operations in Ukraine.
